sections.paragraph:
ὀφείλω [cp. ὀφλήσω the fut. of ὀφλισϰάνω ‘become a debtor’]

—1. ‘be under prescribed obligation’, owe Mt 18:28; Lk 7:41; 16:5; Phlm 18; do one’s duty, as expected of a slave Lk 17:10; of consequence under a legal system J 19:7. Wordplay in Mt 23:18; Ro 13:8 forms a bridge to

—2. ‘be under interpersonal obligation’, owe Lk 11:4; Ro 15:1; one ought J 13:14; Ac 17:29; be obliged 1 Cor 5:10; 2 Cor 12:14; Eph 5:28; 2 Th 1:3; Hb 2:17; 1 J 2:6; 3 J 8.
